definition: The direct, single-going quality of mind that goes straight to the work and the goal without scattering into side tracks, associated with steadiness, samādhi, and jhāna.
notes: The project promotes this entry to a major rule-bearing record because ekaggatā is too important to leave as a thin definitional stub. The project now keeps directness as the default because, in practice, ekaggatā is not mere one-pointed staring. In first jhāna and in ānāpānasati as this repository understands it, ekaggatā is the not-beating-around-the-bush quality that goes straight toward dukkha-nirodha: distractions are set aside, wholesome dhammas are brought up, and the line of practice is kept heading in the right direction. Singleness remains acceptable when the noun needs a slightly more literal tone, and unification can still be used when the relation to the formula term cetaso ekodibhāva needs to be foregrounded. One-pointedness should not drive the lexicon, because it pulls the term toward a narrow concentration model and away from the practical directness that matters in the first mental theme. In DN 2 and MN 19, this family stays close to jhāna and samādhi as workable gatheredness rather than as a trance theory. The OSF glossary still supports the broader practical direction, but the repository now encodes that direction more sharply through directness and single aim language.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and meditative contexts
When the relation to cetaso ekodibhāva or gathered samādhi needs to be explicit
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate in explanatory prose.

context_rules.context: When a slightly more literal noun is needed without losing the practical force
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when directness would read too interpretively for the line.

context_rules.context: In first-jhāna and ānāpānasati explanation where the point is not beating around the bush
context_rules.notes: Use this when the line needs explicit goal-directed practical texture.

example_phrases.notes: Closely related phrase that helps explain the family meaning.

example_phrases.translation: a mentally composed mind, direct in aim
example_phrases.notes: Shows the close relation of ekaggatā and samādhi.

version_notes: Promoted from a minor stub to a rule-bearing major entry and later sharpened to prefer directness over one-pointedness in first-jhāna and ānāpānasati handling.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping the term practical and samadhi-adjacent without collapsing it into concentration-style one-pointedness.
Keeps the family close to jhāna and samādhi as practical gatheredness in the standard fourfold sequence.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'directness' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'one-pointedness', 'concentration', or other unrecorded alternates.
